input:
He said: “You would have been well advised to use different language.” The Muslim delegation then left the Qurayẓah and went back to the Prophet with the bad news that the Jews no longer recognized the peace treaty which they had with him. On arrival Saʿd ibn Muʿādh and Saʿd ibn ʿUbādah found the Prophet with a group of his companions. Following his advice that they should give him a hint of the Qurayẓah’s treachery rather than give the fact full publicity, they mentioned in their report the names of the two Arab tribes, ʿAḍal and al-Qārah, in reference to the treachery they had perpetrated at al-Rajīʿ.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 25: A Fresh Storm Gathers

output.scene_title: The Muslim Delegation Returns with Bad News
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 25: A Fresh Storm Gathers

characters.name: Saʿd ibn Muʿādh
characters.appearance: Late 30s, light brown skin, athletic build, strong facial features. Thick dark beard, neatly trimmed. Wearing a beige woolen cloak over a brown thobe, white headscarf wrapped around head. Simple leather sandals. Warm and concerned expression.

characters.name: Saʿd ibn ʿUbādah
characters.appearance: Mid 30s, medium brown skin, muscular build, rugged facial features. Dark beard with slight grey streaks, neatly kept. Wearing a dark brown woolen cloak over a beige thobe, white headscarf wrapped around head. Simple leather sandals. Confident and determined expression.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

output.moral_lesson: Honesty is always the best policy, even in difficult situations.
output.narrative_text: The Muslim delegation returned to the Prophet Muhammad with bad news from the Qurayẓah tribe. They had broken the peace treaty and no longer recognized the Prophet. Saʿd ibn Muʿādh and Saʿd ibn ʿUbādah advised the Prophet to keep this information private, at least for now.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, with his companions. No graphic content or conflicts. Modest clothing for all characters.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Exterior of a 7th century Makkah mud-brick home with a group of men standing in the foreground. A man in a white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing in the center, surrounded by three men in beige and brown woolen cloaks, white headscarves wrapped around heads. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
